title: mount a dir to mergeddir but container not found the dir
body:
### Issue Description

I'm not sure if it's caused by podman, but I didn't encounter this problem under docker and docker-compose.
The premise is that I need to mount a directory on the host into an already running container. My method is to find the mergeddir of the container, and then mount the directory to mergeddir. The mount is successful and the mount point information can be found on the host, but the container is not mounted successfully.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue

in host:
run:
`mount --bind /tmp/test1 /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/e6f3c9c73e1a80f03a6fcccac32c0ed4f543f2a0544a57cd4302103eb0b46aee/merged/tmp/test1`
run success 
and 
```
ls -li /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/e6f3c9c73e1a80f03a6fcccac32c0ed4f543f2a0544a57cd4302103eb0b46aee/merged/tmp/test1
522978 -rw-r--r-- 1 root root 0 Feb  7 19:02 aaa
```




### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received:
in continer:
`ls -l /tmp/test1`
empty
and run mount | grep  /tmp/test1 no result

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected
in continer:
`ls -li /tmp/test1/
522978 -rw-r--r-- 1 root root 0 Feb   7 19:02 aaa`

### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

It probably has nothing to do with mirroring. I tried multiple mirrors and this happened all the time.

comments.body: You need to check the mount propagation `findmnt -R -o TARGET,PROPAGATION /`, by default `/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay` will be made private by podman, there is a `skip_mount_home=true` storage option to disable it.

thanks, but according to the information I found, overlay fs has given up support for skip_mount_home：https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/480 and the containers has also removed this item. Why podman still uses and supports this feature?
I tried changing it to skip_mount_home="true", and then restarted podman and the container image, but it didn't take effect. mergedddir is still privately mounted.

Let me introduce the background: my process runs on the host, and i need to inject the process in the container and communicate across containers. The way i used to do it was to mount the socket file into the container. Is there any other way to achieve this without modifying the user's container configuration?
